audits.cleanReason: Re-read the prose for true contrast-style negation pivots and found only three real candidates; each survived as tactical binary diagnosis or hard constraint rather than decorative not-X scaffolding.

audits.dominantPattern: body-part and scene-state shorthand after the pass-a P5/P6 sweep
clusterFlags: Late exit handoff paragraph (lines 585-587) contains 2 inanimate-motion shorthand KILLs.

secondSweep.note: Performed the required P5/P6 full-sentence reread for inanimate subjects carrying agency or physical-behavior verbs; it surfaced the two exit-paragraph motion-shorthand KILLs below.

findings.line: The captain found his voice first.
findings.subject: voice
findings.realAgent: the captain speaking once he gets control of himself
findings.reason: Pattern 8 body-part agency: the captain is the actor, not his voice.

findings.line: The chamber passed out of sight behind her.
findings.subject: chamber
findings.realAgent: Jaina and the guards moving away from the throne room
findings.reason: The sentence launders the movement onto the chamber instead of naming the escort's motion.

findings.line: The walls turned to service stone almost at once.
findings.subject: walls
findings.realAgent: the route shifting from ceremonial marble into service corridors
findings.reason: The architecture is given active behavior instead of the prose naming the visible material change directly.
findings.suggestedFix: Service stone replaced marble almost at once.

audits.cleanReason: Counted all seven tic families. 'marked' appears once in a physical targeting use, tightened/eased stay under cap with enough literal uses, and the eye/gaze beats that actually function as reaction beats stay at the hard cap rather than exceeding it.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A lands at three mild KILLs total, all local intent-smear shorthand with conservative drop-in fixes cross-checked against the full pass-A guide pack.
